div.h1: One-Shot vs Repeating Timer: Heap State Before and After Expiry

div.p: One-Shot Result: Timer removed from heap BEFORE cb fires. cb calling reactor_cancel_timer(42) → returns -1 → harmless. No ghost entries. No double-free risk. Heap property restored by sift_down in O(log N).
heap[0] → pool[slot] timer_id = 42 expiry_ms = 1000 ≤ now_ms() interval_ms = 0 ← one-shot heap_idx = 0 cb = on_idle_timeout udata = conn*
heap[size-1] → pool[slot_last] timer_id = 77 expiry_ms = 45000 interval_ms = 0 heap_idx = size-1
① Capture before mutation tid ← pool[slot].timer_id = 42 interval ← pool[slot].interval_ms = 0 cb ← pool[slot].callback udata ← pool[slot].udata
② interval_ms == 0 → REMOVE heap_cancel_at_slot(r, slot) swap heap[0] ↔ heap[size-1] timer_heap_size-- sift_down(r, 0) memset(pool[slot], 0)
③ Call cb(tid=42, udata) on_idle_timeout fires cb may call: reactor_cancel_timer(r, 42) → linear scan finds NO active entry with timer_id=42 → returns -1 (safe no-op)
heap[0] → pool[slot_last] (promoted) timer_id = 77 expiry_ms = 45000 heap_idx = 0 ← updated by sift_down
pool[slot] — CLEARED memset → all zeros active = 0 timer_id = 0 (slot available for reuse)
